ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'salad food product' class (FOODON_00002473). No specific code exists for a customisable egg salad with three toppings and no dressing; closest_parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers the 'Salads' category. No specific facet code for egg-topped customisable salad without dressing; closest_parent category match applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for customisable egg salad without dressing. SNOMED CT clinical food coding does not extend to this level of menu specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of salad with egg and three toppings, no sauce, weighing approximately 250 g, as typically served at a Singapore hawker stall or café.

nutrient_source.title: Salad with egg (3 toppings), no sauce — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Foodo record F08010046 for 'Salad with egg (3 toppings), no sauce'. All per-100 g values are as reported; confidence 0.95 per source metadata.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific customisable egg salad. GI estimated at 32 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for mixed green salad with vegetables (GI ~32–38 range) and the dominant low-GI ingredients: raw leafy vegetables, egg, and no added dressing. Carbohydrate content is very low (2.47 g/100 g); glycemic load per 250 g serving calculated as (32 × 2.47 × 2.5) / 100 ≈ 2.0 (negligible). Analogous estimate; direct measurement recommended for clinical use.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (125 mg/100 g) with no added dressing, aligning with DASH dietary principles
hypertension.guidance: At 125 mg sodium per 100 g and with dressing omitted, this salad is well-suited for hypertension management; patients should select toppings that avoid processed meats, pickled items, or high-sodium cheeses to preserve the low-sodium profile.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (2.47 g/100 g), low GI (~32), and egg protein slowing gastric emptying
type2_diabetes.guidance: With only 2.47 g carbohydrate per 100 g, a low estimated GI of approximately 32, and a negligible glycemic load of ~2.0 per serving, this salad poses minimal postprandial glucose risk; patients with Type 2 Diabetes should limit starchy toppings (croutons, corn, potato) to maintain the low glycaemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~0.6 g/100 g), low energy density (42 kcal/100 g), and high vegetable content supporting heart-healthy eating
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low energy density, low saturated fat, and absence of dressing make this salad consistent with heart-healthy dietary patterns; patients with cardiovascular disease should avoid high-fat or processed-meat toppings and be mindful that egg yolk contributes dietary cholesterol, though current evidence supports moderate egg consumption within a balanced diet.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Absence of confirmed anti-inflammatory nutrients (omega-3, polyphenols) at meaningful levels; neutrality is insufficient for RA benefit
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: While this salad is low in pro-inflammatory components, it lacks confirmed anti-inflammatory factors at therapeutic levels; patients with rheumatoid arthritis would benefit from selecting anti-inflammatory toppings such as walnuts or flaxseeds, and should monitor individual tolerance to nightshade vegetables (tomato, bell pepper).

llm_training.natural_language_description: Salad with Egg (3 Toppings), No Sauce is a customisable fresh salad dish commonly served at Singapore hawker centres, food courts, and cafés. It consists of a base of mixed fresh vegetables — typically including leafy greens such as lettuce, cucumber slices, cherry tomatoes, and shredded cabbage — topped with one whole or sliced hard-boiled or soft-boiled egg and a customer-selected combination of three additional toppings, which may include items such as corn kernels, croutons, tuna, chickpeas, shredded chicken, or other available options. No dressing or sauce is added, making this one of the lowest-calorie and lowest-sodium salad variants available in the Singapore food service context. Per 100 g, the dish provides only 42 kcal, 3.34 g protein, 2.15 g fat, and 2.47 g carbohydrate, with 1.39 g dietary fibre and 125 mg sodium. The egg contributes high-quality complete protein and micronutrients including iron (0.85 mg/100 g) and calcium (36 mg/100 g). The absence of dressing significantly reduces caloric density and sodium load compared to dressed salad variants. Clinically, this dish is well-suited for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes (very low GI ~32, negligible glycemic load), hypertension (low sodium, DASH-compatible), and cardiovascular disease (low saturated fat, low energy density). The texture is crisp and fresh; flavour is mild and neutral without dressing. This dish is vegetarian, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free by default, though topping choices may alter these flags.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database record F08010046 with confidence 0.95 per source metadata. Exact match to the named dish. GI is an analogous estimate (low confidence for GI sub-component only) but all macros and micronutrients are from an approved primary source.
notes: 1. saturated_fat_g estimated at 0.6 g/100 g based on typical egg and vegetable fat composition (HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this record); this is an approximation and should be updated when HPB publishes disaggregated fatty acid data. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific customisable salad variant are not reported in the HPB Foodo database (F08010046) as of 2026-04-16. 3. GI value of 32 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for mixed green salads; direct GI measurement for this specific dish is not available. Glycemic load per 250 g serving ≈ 2.0 (negligible). 4. Topping composition is variable by customer choice; nutrient values reflect the HPB canonical composite record and may vary depending on actual topping selection. 5. dietary_tags from input include 'keto' — this is defensible given the very low carbohydrate content (2.47 g/100 g) but may vary with topping choices.
